cases.description: Basic sum of a contiguous numeric range
A text value stored in a referenced cell is IGNORED by SUM, not coerced to a number, even though it looks numeric
cases.expected_note: A2 holds the text string '2' (not the number 2); SUM over a range skips text cells entirely, so the total is 1+3=4, not 6

cases.description: A numeric-looking TEXT LITERAL passed directly as a function argument (not via a cell reference) IS coerced to a number, unlike the same text sitting in a referenced cell
cases.expected_note: Contrast with SUM_text_in_cell_reference_ignored: Excel coerces literal string arguments that look like numbers when they are typed directly into the formula, but does not do so for text found inside a range reference

cases.description: Summing an entirely empty range returns 0, not an error
